repo.name: evolutionary-architecture/evolutionary-architecture-by-example
repo.stack: .NET 10 / C# (Domain-Driven Design): modular monolith (Offers/Passes/Reports) + an extracted Contracts microservice (Clean Architecture) + shared Fitnet.Common libraries

reference_claims.text: The system is organized into bounded-context modules, each with its own namespace: Passes, Offers, Reports in the monolith (Contracts has been extracted as a separate service per ADR-0018).

Modules do not reference other modules' implementation projects (Api/DataAccess/Core); the only inter-module production project coupling is referencing a sender module's *.IntegrationEvents project (the ADR-0014 carve-out). Each module is otherwise independent/self-contained.
A single relational database is divided into a separate schema per module; each module's data is encapsulated in its own schema (Offers -> 'Offers', Passes -> 'Passes', Contracts -> 'Contracts').
Code is organized in vertical slices (each business process in its own namespace/folder), eliminating traditional technical folders like Controllers, Entities, Commands, Queries.
The application is containerized with Docker (Dockerfiles present for the deployable units).
Classes are 'internal sealed' by default (accessible only within their assembly, sealed against inheritance) unless there is a specific reason to be public/open.
The solution includes both unit-test projects and integration-test projects (the combined testing strategy).
Offers and Passes integrate via asynchronous messaging (not synchronous in-process calls): when a pass expires, Offers consumes the Passes integration event PassExpiredEvent.
Static code analysis is enabled (analyzers on, warnings treated as errors, code style enforced in build).
The Contracts module is structured into four Clean-Architecture layers as separate projects: Api, Application, Infrastructure, Core.
Core is the heart of the Contracts module: it contains the domain model and essential business logic.
Application contains the use cases specific to the Contracts module (command + handler pairs).
Infrastructure handles technical concerns: persistence, configuration, external-system integration, I/O.
Api handles external communication (HTTP requests) and exposes the Contracts module's interface.

Clean Architecture enforces clear directional dependencies between layers: Core is the innermost layer and depends on nothing internal (no reference to Api/Application/Infrastructure); dependencies point inward toward Core.
The Passes module follows a layered approach split into two projects: an API project and a DataAccess project.
In Passes, the API layer handles external communication while the DataAccess layer handles persistence and the (simple) business logic (the Pass entity behavior + the DbContext).
The Reports module uses a transaction-script / simple read-model style (raw SQL reads via Dapper over a single project), not a rich domain model.
Each event-sending module owns a dedicated *.IntegrationEvents project holding its integration-event definitions (Passes.IntegrationEvents, Contracts.IntegrationEvents).
A receiving module references the sender's *.IntegrationEvents project/package to reuse the event definitions (Offers.Api -> Passes.IntegrationEvents; Passes.Api -> Contracts.IntegrationEvents).
Contracts implements Command-Query Separation using the MediatR library (ICommand : IRequest, IRequestHandler).
Modules can be enabled/disabled via configuration (a 'Modules:<Name>:Enabled' flag checked at registration), which integration tests use to isolate the module under test.

The Contracts module has been extracted from the monolith into a separate, independently-deployable microservice (own solution Fitnet.Contracts.slnx, own AppHost + Dockerfile), and is no longer one of the monolith's in-process modules.

The monolith no longer references the Contracts module by a project reference; the only coupling from the monolith to Contracts is consuming Contracts' integration events (Passes.Api references the Contracts.IntegrationEvents PACKAGE, not a project).
Inter-service / cross-boundary communication uses an out-of-process message queue (MassTransit over a broker), not an in-memory bus.
The message broker is RabbitMQ (MassTransit UsingRabbitMq + Aspire RabbitMQ hosting).
The in-memory event bus has been fully discontinued: no in-memory event-bus implementation remains in production; the out-of-process MassTransit/RabbitMQ bus is the only event bus.
The Inbox-Outbox pattern is implemented for reliable messaging, with inbox/outbox state persisted in the database.
The Inbox-Outbox is implemented using MassTransit's built-in EntityFrameworkCore mechanism (AddEntityFrameworkOutbox), not a hand-rolled custom solution.
Contracts uses a Result type (the ErrorOr library) for business-flow error handling instead of throwing exceptions for negative business scenarios.
BindingContract is the Aggregate Root of the Contracts domain (derives from Entity, private ctor + static Start factory, owns the AttachedAnnexes child collection, records domain events).
Contracts uses tactical-DDD building blocks: a shared Entity base class (with domain events) plus value objects / strongly-typed IDs (BindingContractId, ContractId, AnnexId).
Tests use Shouldly as the assertion library (the project migrated to Shouldly).

emitted_claims.text: Fitnet is a modular monolith: one ASP.NET Core host composes feature modules (Offers/Passes/Reports), each owning a PostgreSQL schema in one shared DB, talking over an in-process + RabbitMQ bus.
Fitnet.Contracts is an extracted Clean/Onion microservice (Core/Application/Infrastructure/Api) with its own host and own DB.
Fitnet.Common is five shared libs published as NuGet packages EvolutionaryArchitecture.Fitnet.Common.* v4.2.0, consumed by both apps.
Stack: EF Core 10 + Npgsql/PostgreSQL, Dapper (Reports only), MediatR, MassTransit+RabbitMQ, FluentValidation, ErrorOr, Scalar OpenAPI, .NET Aspire (AppHost); versions pinned centrally.
contracts-service is the gym-contract lifecycle microservice (Prepare/Sign/Annex/Terminate) that publishes ContractSignedEvent.
passes provisions a Pass on contract-sign and owns the monolith's MassTransit + EF outbox.
offers is an event-only module that prepares a renewal Offer when a Pass expires (no HTTP).
reports is Dapper read-side analytics that reads the Passes table directly via SQL.
monolith-host is the Fitnet host plus the two Aspire AppHosts (Postgres+RabbitMQ).
common-shared provides Entity/ValueObject, the BusinessRule engine, error->HTTP mapping, clock, mediator, and module gating.
monolith host depends on offers, passes, reports (ProjectReference).
offers depends on passes.IntegrationEvents (compile-time) plus offers.DataAccess.
passes depends on passes.DataAccess plus passes.IntegrationEvents.
reports depends on the Passes DB table directly (SQL, no code ref).
contracts host -> contracts.Api -> {core, infrastructure, application}; application -> {core, integrationEvents}; infrastructure -> {application, core} forming an Onion DAG.
monolith/passes consumes the microservice's ContractSignedEvent over RabbitMQ via NuGet package.
all feature modules depend on Common.* (NuGet).

Two coupling mechanisms exist: ProjectReference (in-app, compile-time) vs PackageReference (NuGet: all Common.* + cross-app Contracts.IntegrationEvents); the Common source tree is NOT in either app's build graph.
PostgreSQL; monolith is one DB fitnetsdb with schemas Passes/Offers (Reports reads Passes); microservice has its own DB fitnetcontractsdb with schema Contracts.
Tables: Passes (Passes.Passes); Offers (Offers.Offers); Contracts/BindingContracts/Annexes (schema Contracts); plus MassTransit Inbox/Outbox tables in the Passes schema only.
Per-module connection-string config (<Module>:Database:ConnectionString); EF migrations run automatically at startup via Database.Migrate(), no separate deploy step.

The end-to-end business flow is an async event chain across the deployment boundary: Contracts.Sign -> ContractSignedEvent(RabbitMQ) -> Passes.Register; Passes.Expire -> PassExpiredEvent -> Offers.PrepareOffer; no synchronous call between the apps.

Passes is the monolith's only MassTransit registrar (AddEventBus + scans its own assembly); Offers does not register the bus and only works because Passes is added first in Program.cs; reordering/disabling Passes breaks Offers' consumer + IPublishEndpoint.

Outbox asymmetry: the monolith's Passes uses an EF transactional outbox (30s dedup window); the Contracts microservice has no outbox - SignContractCommandHandler publishes AFTER commit, so the event is lost on a crash between the two.

Reports -> Passes coupling is through the database, not code: a raw Dapper FROM "Passes"."Passes" query; a Passes schema change silently breaks Reports at query time with nothing at compile time to catch it.

Business rules are the system spine: domain invariants are IBusinessRules composed by BusinessRuleValidator (returns ErrorOr, type 100); every business-rule failure surfaces as HTTP 409 Conflict via ProblemResults; the namespace is misspelled BussinessRules.
Contract invariants: adult age >= 18, height <= 210 cm, sign within 30 days of prepare, 365-day duration, 3-month termination lock-in.

Config/container drift: Contracts' EventBusModule binds section "EventBus" but its appsettings define "ExternalEventBus" (only the AppHost-injected EventBus__Uri lines up); Dockerfiles build on .NET 8/9 images though the code targets net10.0.
PackageReference is used to consume Fitnet.Common.* and the Fitnet.Contracts.IntegrationEvents public event contract; the monolith depends on the microservice's events as a package, not a project.
The Common source tree (Fitnet.Common/) is NOT in either app's project graph - it is pre-built and pulled from a GitHub NuGet feed.
Each app has an Aspire AppHost orchestrator (Postgres + RabbitMQ) for local run.
Fitnet host references Offers.Api, Passes.Api, Reports.
Offers.Api references Passes.IntegrationEvents and Offers.DataAccess (cross-module edge).
Passes.Api references Passes.DataAccess and Passes.IntegrationEvents.
Reports has no project refs; it reads other modules' data via raw SQL.
Fitnet.Contracts host references Contracts.Api.
Contracts.Api references Contracts.Core, Contracts.Infrastructure, Contracts.Application.
Contracts.Application references Contracts.Core and Contracts.IntegrationEvents.
Contracts.Infrastructure references Contracts.Application and Contracts.Core.
The Contracts edges form a strict DAG (Core is the leaf; Api composes Infrastructure+Application+Core) in the classic Onion direction; no project-reference cycles.
Offers.Api consumes PassExpiredEvent via a compile-time ProjectReference to Passes.IntegrationEvents; a change to that event's shape breaks Offers at compile time.

The monolith's Passes module consumes the microservice's ContractSignedEvent (over RabbitMQ) via the EvolutionaryArchitecture.Fitnet.Contracts.IntegrationEvents NuGet package; version pinned in Directory.Packages.props.
Package versions are pinned centrally via Directory.Packages.props (Central Package Management, ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true).
MediatR 12.5.0 provides in-process command/query + notification dispatch.
MassTransit.RabbitMQ 8.3.2 is the message bus transport.
MassTransit.EntityFrameworkCore 8.3.2 provides the EF transactional outbox/inbox.
Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore 10.0.1 + Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL 10.0.0 provide the ORM + PG provider.
Dapper 2.1.66 provides raw-SQL read access (Reports only).
FluentValidation 12.0.0 provides request validation.
ErrorOr (via Common) is the result/error value type (no exceptions for business errors).
Scalar.AspNetCore 2.12.17 provides the OpenAPI UI at /docs/v1.
Aspire.Hosting.PostgreSQL / RabbitMQ 13.1.0 provide local orchestration (AppHost only).

Build/runtime-target inconsistency: all csproj target net10.0 but the Dockerfiles build/run on older SDK/runtime images (monolith uses dotnet/sdk:9.0 + aspnet:9.0, Contracts uses 8.0); the container build path is stale relative to the code's target framework.
Monolith Passes uses schema Passes with DbContext PassesPersistence (EF Core), tables Passes plus MassTransit InboxState/OutboxMessage/OutboxState.
Monolith Offers uses schema Offers with DbContext OffersPersistence (EF Core), table Offers.
Microservice Contracts uses schema Contracts with DbContext ContractsPersistence (EF Core), tables Contracts, BindingContracts, Annexes.
Monolith DB is fitnetsdb (db name fitnet), injected as Database__ConnectionString; RabbitMQ as EventBus__ConnectionString.
Microservice DB is fitnetcontractsdb (db name fitnet), injected as Database__ConnectionString; RabbitMQ as EventBus__Uri.
Both AppHosts spin up Postgres 14.3 + RabbitMQ.
Each module reads its own nested config section (Passes:Database:ConnectionString) so connection strings are configured per module, not once.
Offers reads Offers:Database:ConnectionString.
Reports reads Reports:Database:ConnectionString.
Microservice reads Database:ConnectionString.
Every EF module calls context.Database.Migrate() during host startup via AutomaticMigrationsExtensions.UseAutomaticMigrations(); there is no separate deploy-time migration step.
Contract PK Id is a value object ContractId(Guid) with an EF value converter; Signature is an EF owned type stored as Signature_Date/Signature_Value (varchar(100)) on the Contracts table.
BindingContract is a separate table with ContractId foreign reference, Duration (interval), BindingFrom, ExpiringAt, nullable TerminatedAt.
Annex is an owned collection of BindingContract, own table Annexes, composite key (BindingContractId, Id), cascade-delete FK to BindingContracts.
Pass table Passes in schema Passes has columns Id, CustomerId, From, To; all required; To is the only mutable column (set on expiry).
Offer table Offers in schema Offers has columns Id, CustomerId, PreparedAt, Discount (numeric), OfferedFromDate, OfferedFromTo; all required; immutable (init-only).
Passes has 2 migrations: CreatePassesTable (20230503180338) and AddOutbox (20231107192159, adds Inbox/Outbox tables).
Offers has 1 migration: CreateOffersTable (20230503180337).
Contracts has 10 migrations (May-2023 to Oct-2024) tracing the model's evolution, ending with replace SignedAt with owned Signature.

The monolith's Passes module wires a MassTransit EF transactional outbox over PassesPersistence (UsePostgres, UseBusOutbox, 30-second duplicate-detection window); integration events are staged in the OutboxMessage table in the same transaction as the data write, then dispatched.

The Contracts microservice does NOT use an outbox; SignContractCommandHandler commits the DB transaction and THEN calls publishEndpoint.Publish(ContractSignedEvent) as a separate step, so the event is lost if the process dies after commit but before publish; there are no Inbox/Outbox migrations in the Contracts schema.

Reports runs Dapper SQL directly against another module's table FROM "Passes"."Passes", grouping pass registrations per month of "From" for the current UTC year; the year is interpolated from TimeProvider.GetUtcNow().Year (server-derived int, not user input).
Reports' DatabaseConnectionFactory is a singleton that creates/reuses one NpgsqlConnection.
Contract is a membership agreement drafted for a customer; lifecycle Prepared -> Signed -> (expires after Duration); standard Duration = 365 days.
BindingContract is the active agreement created when a Contract is signed; can have Annexes attached and can be terminated after a 3-month lock-in.
Annex is a plan modification attached to an active BindingContract.
Signature (VO) accepts letters+spaces only.
Pass is a membership pass for a customer over [From, To], provisioned when a Contract is signed; can be expired early.
Offer is a renewal incentive (standard 10% discount, 1-year validity) prepared when a Pass expires.

There is no shared Customer aggregate; CustomerId (Guid) is the linking identifier carried on every aggregate and across every event (ContractSigned -> Pass.Register uses ContractCustomerId; PassExpired -> Offer uses CustomerId).
WF-001: POST /api/contracts -> PrepareContractCommand -> Contract.Prepare (age>=18, height<=210, previous-signed rules) -> persist.

WF-001: PATCH /api/contracts/{id} -> SignContractCommand -> Contract.Sign (not-already-signed, within-30-days; builds Signature VO) -> create BindingContract, persist -> publish ContractSignedEvent to RabbitMQ.

WF-002: ContractSignedEvent (RabbitMQ) -> monolith ContractSignedEventConsumer -> Pass.Register(ContractCustomerId, SignedAt, ExpireAt) -> persist -> publish PassRegisteredEvent; delivered through the Passes EF outbox; consumer retries 3x immediately + scheduled redeliveries.
WF-003: PATCH /api/passes/{id} -> Pass.MarkAsExpired(now) -> persist -> publish PassExpiredEvent.
WF-003: PassExpiredEvent -> Offers.PassExpiredEventConsumer -> Offer.PrepareStandardPassExtension(CustomerId, now) (10% / 1yr) -> persist -> publish OfferPrepareEvent.
WF-004: POST /api/binding-contracts/{id}/annexes -> AttachAnnex (active + valid-from within period).
WF-004: PATCH /api/binding-contracts/{id}/terminate -> Terminate (only after 3-month lock-in).
WF-005: GET {api}/reports/generate -> Dapper SQL over "Passes"."Passes" grouped by month for the current year -> NewPassesRegistrationsPerMonthResponse.
Contracts host entry/composition root: AddContractsApi(configuration) -> AddInfrastructure(...).

Contract aggregate factory Prepare(customerId, customerAge, customerHeight, preparedAt, isPreviousContractSigned?) returns ErrorOr<Contract>; standard duration = TimeSpan.FromDays(365); Sign(signature, now) returns ErrorOr<BindingContract>, sets ExpiringAt = now + Duration.
Signature value object validates against regex ^[A-Za-z\s]+$, else SignatureNotValidException.
ContractCanBePreparedOnlyForAdultRule enforces age >= 18.
CustomerMustBeSmallerThanMaximumHeightLimitRule enforces height <= 210 cm (gym-instrument limit).
PreviousContractHasToBeSignedRule requires a prior contract (if any) to be signed.
ContractMustNotBeAlreadySignedRule enforces sign once only.
ContractCanOnlyBeSignedWithin30DaysFromPreparationRule enforces sign within 30 days of prepare (date-only).
AnnexCanOnlyBeAttachedToActiveBindingContractRule enforces not terminated & not expired.
AnnexCanOnlyStartDuringBindingContractPeriodRule enforces annex validFrom <= expiry.
TerminationIsPossibleOnlyAfterThreeMonthsHavePassedRule enforces a 3-month lock-in before terminate.
Rules are composed via BusinessRuleValidator.Validate(rule1, rule2, ...) from Common.Core; failures short-circuit to an ErrorOr error of type 100 (BusinessRuleError).

Application layer commands: PrepareContractCommand, SignContractCommand, AttachAnnexToBindingContractCommand, TerminateBindingContractCommand, each with a CommandHandler; ports IContractsRepository, IBindingContractsRepository expose GetByIdAsync/AddAsync/CommitAsync.
POST /api/contracts maps to ContractsApiPaths.Prepare.
PATCH /api/contracts/{id} maps to ContractsApiPaths.Sign.
POST /api/binding-contracts/{id}/annexes maps to ContractsApiPaths.AttachAnnex.
PATCH /api/binding-contracts/{id}/terminate maps to ContractsApiPaths.Terminate.

Endpoints validate requests with FluentValidation (Signature NotEmpty + MaxLength 100); errors map to HTTP via ProblemResults (BusinessRuleError->409, Validation->400, NotFound->404, Failure/Unexpected->500).

ContractsPersistence DbContext uses schema "Contracts", DbSet<Contract> + DbSet<BindingContract>, EF value-converters for id value objects, owned Signature, owned Annexes collection (cascade delete), 10 migrations, PostgreSQL via UseNpgsql.

ContractSignedEvent(Id, ContractId, ContractCustomerId, SignedAt, ExpireAt, OccurredDateTime) is the service's public bus contract published in SignContractCommandHandler after commit, consumed by the monolith's Passes module; the four domain events (ContractPrepared, BindingContractStarted, AnnexAttached, BindingContractTerminated) are internal-only.
Contracts repositories commit eagerly (AddAsync + CommitAsync / direct SaveChanges) with no unit-of-work spanning the request.
The Contracts schema name "Contracts" is hardcoded, not configurable.
The Contracts Dockerfile builds on .NET 8 images while csproj targets net10.0.
Passes module registration: AddPasses(configuration, module) (DI; gated by IsModuleEnabled) and RegisterPasses(module) (pipeline/endpoints), called from host Program.cs:16,37.
Passes HTTP: GET /api/passes (PassesApiPaths.GetAll) and PATCH /api/passes/{id} mark-as-expired (PassesApiPaths.MarkPassAsExpired).
Passes event consumer ContractSignedEventConsumer : IConsumer<ContractSignedEvent>.
Pass has Id, CustomerId, From, To (init-only except To); factory Register(customerId, from, to); MarkAsExpired(now) sets To = now; no creation-time invariant on From < To.
PassesPersistence DbContext uses schema Passes, table Passes, 2 migrations (CreatePassesTable, AddOutbox with Inbox/Outbox tables), auto-migrate on startup.

Passes consumes ContractSignedEvent (from Contracts microservice over RabbitMQ via the Contracts.IntegrationEvents package); on receipt Pass.Register(ContractCustomerId, SignedAt, ExpireAt), persist, then publish PassRegisteredEvent.
Passes publishes PassExpiredEvent(Id, PassId, CustomerId, OccurredDateTime) on mark-as-expired, consumed in-process by Offers.
Passes consumer retry/redelivery policy: 3 immediate retries (1s) + scheduled redeliveries.

Passes AddEventBus configures MassTransit + RabbitMQ + the EF transactional outbox (AddEntityFrameworkOutbox<PassesPersistence>, UsePostgres, UseBusOutbox, 30s duplicate-detection), AddConsumers(executing assembly) and SetSnakeCaseEndpointNameFormatter; bus connects only when EventBus:Uri is non-empty.
Passes.Api PackageRefs: Common.Api, Common.Core, Contracts.IntegrationEvents, MassTransit.*; ProjectRefs: Passes.DataAccess, Passes.IntegrationEvents.

Passes is the monolith's MassTransit host; its AddEventBus registers the bus + scans the executing assembly for consumers; Offers does NOT call AddEventBus and relies on Passes being registered first in Program.cs (16 before 17) so IPublishEndpoint resolves.
GetAllPasses projects only Id+CustomerId (not From/To); read-only AsNoTracking.
MarkAsExpired has no guard against re-expiring an already-expired pass; it just resets To to now.
Offers module registration: AddOffers(configuration, module) / RegisterOffers(module) (both gated by IsModuleEnabled), called from host Program.cs:17,38.
Offers' only runtime entry is the event consumer PassExpiredEventConsumer : IConsumer<PassExpiredEvent>; no route mapping exists.

Offer is immutable (init-only) Id, CustomerId, PreparedAt, Discount, OfferedFromDate, OfferedFromTo; factory PrepareStandardPassExtension(customerId, nowDate) with hardcoded 10% discount (const decimal standardDiscount = 0.1m), valid now -> now.AddYears(1).
OffersPersistence DbContext uses schema Offers, table Offers, 1 migration CreateOffersTable, auto-migrate on startup.

Offers consumes PassExpiredEvent (from Passes module, in-process bus, via compile-time ProjectReference to Passes.IntegrationEvents); on receipt creates Offer, persists, then publishes OfferPrepareEvent(Id, OfferId, CustomerId, OccurredDateTime).
Offers.Api PackageRef Common.Core; ProjectRefs Passes.IntegrationEvents, Offers.DataAccess.
Offers has compile-time coupling to Passes via the Passes.IntegrationEvents project reference (not a package); a change to PassExpiredEvent breaks Offers at build.
Offers has no own event-bus registration; its consumer + IPublishEndpoint only work because the Passes module registered MassTransit (implicit ordering coupling).
Offers' 10% discount and 1-year validity are hardcoded, not configurable.
No transactional outbox in Offers; publish is a separate step after SaveChanges.
Reports module registration: AddReports(configuration, module) / RegisterReports(module) (gated by IsModuleEnabled), called from host Program.cs:18,39.
Reports HTTP: GET {api}/reports/generate (ReportsApiPaths).
Reports uses IDatabaseConnectionFactory / DatabaseConnectionFactory (singleton; one reused NpgsqlConnection); config section Reports:Database.
NewPassesRegistrationPerMonthReportDataRetriever runs Dapper SQL FROM "Passes"."Passes", grouping by month of "From" filtered to EXTRACT(YEAR ...) = TimeProvider.GetUtcNow().Year.
Reports.csproj PackageRefs: Common.Api, Common.Core, Common.Infrastructure, Dapper, Npgsql, MassTransit.RabbitMQ.
Reports cross-module DB coupling: reads the Passes module's "Passes"."Passes" table directly via SQL (no API or event); a Passes schema change silently breaks the report at query time.
MassTransit.RabbitMQ is referenced by Reports but Reports has no consumers/publishers - currently unused.

Monolith host (Fitnet/Src/Fitnet) is the ASP.NET Core composition root: registers controllers, OpenAPI, AddClock, AddPasses/AddOffers/AddReports, maps OpenAPI + Scalar at /docs/v1 in Development, then RegisterPasses/RegisterOffers/RegisterReports.
Module is a typed enum-like record (Offers/Passes/Reports) with an implicit string conversion, used as the module key for config gating.
Registration order is load-bearing: Passes is added before Offers, and Passes owns the MassTransit registration that Offers' consumer depends on.
Microservice host (Fitnet.Contracts) registers TimeProvider.System explicitly + AddClock, then AddContractsApi(configuration); Scalar at /docs/v1; UseErrorHandling; RegisterContractsApi().
Monolith AppHost: Postgres 14.3 (+pgAdmin) DB fitnetsdb, RabbitMQ (+mgmt), wires Database__ConnectionString and EventBus__ConnectionString into the fitnet-modular-monolith project, WaitFor both.
Contracts AppHost: Postgres 14.3 DB fitnetcontractsdb, RabbitMQ, wires Database__ConnectionString and EventBus__Uri into fitnet-contracts.
The two apps each have their own AppHost, DB, and run independently; they integrate only over RabbitMQ (the ContractSignedEvent -> Passes flow).
The monolith AppHost injects the bus as EventBus__ConnectionString but the monolith's EventBusModule binds EventBus:{Uri,Username,Password} - a config-shape mismatch.

Fitnet.Common is five libraries published as EvolutionaryArchitecture.Fitnet.Common.* (version 4.2.0), consumed by both apps as PackageReferences, defining the cross-cutting contracts both apps build on.
Common.Core Entity base tracks domain events; RecordEvent(IDomainEvent) accumulates into Events.
Common.Core ValueObject base provides value equality via GetEqualityComponents().
Common.Core IDomainEvent has Id, OccuredAt.

Common.Core business-rules engine (misspelled namespace BussinessRules): IBusinessRule { bool IsMet(); Error Error; }; BusinessRuleValidator.Validate(params IBusinessRule[]) returns ErrorOr<Success>, collecting the Error of every unmet rule; BusinessRuleError is custom error type 100.
Common.Api ExceptionMiddleware maps ResourceNotFoundException -> 404, everything else -> 500; wired via UseErrorHandling().

Common.Api ProblemResults.ToProblem(errors) is the canonical ErrorOr -> HTTP map: BusinessRuleError(100)->409, Conflict->409, Validation->400, NotFound->404, Failure/Unexpected->500, Unauthorized->401, Forbidden->403.
Common.Api RequestValidationApiFilter<T> + ValidateRequest<T>() is a FluentValidation endpoint filter -> 400 on failure.
Common.Api ApiPaths.Root = "api".
Common.Infrastructure AddClock() registers TimeProvider.System as singleton.
Common.Infrastructure IIntegrationEvent : INotification (Id, OccurredDateTime) + IIntegrationEventHandler<TEvent>.
Common.Infrastructure AddMediator(assembly) -> AddMediatR(RegisterServicesFromAssembly(assembly)).
Common.Infrastructure IsModuleEnabled(config, module) reads Modules:{module}:Enabled.

The business-rule pattern is the system's spine: domain invariants in both apps are IBusinessRules composed by BusinessRuleValidator; every business-rule failure becomes a value (ErrorOr type 100) and surfaces as HTTP 409 Conflict via ProblemResults.
Two error pipelines coexist: legacy ExceptionMiddleware (exceptions -> 404/500) AND the ErrorOr/ProblemResults value path (per-endpoint); a new endpoint must pick the right one.
The namespace is misspelled BussinessRules (double-s) - a real gotcha for using statements.
The Common libs are pulled from a GitHub NuGet feed at build, not built from this tree.

adjudication.referent: The bounded-context module set: Offers/Passes/Reports as monolith modules each with its own namespace, plus Contracts extracted as a separate service.

The module-isolation boundary rule: modules do NOT reference other modules' implementation projects (Api/DataAccess/Core); the ONLY inter-module production project coupling is referencing a sender module's *.IntegrationEvents project (the ADR-0014 carve-out); each module is otherwise independent.
The per-module PostgreSQL schema assignment: Offers->'Offers', Passes->'Passes', Contracts->'Contracts' (each module's data encapsulated in its own schema).
Code-organization principle: vertical slices (each business process in its own namespace/folder) instead of technical folders (Controllers/Entities/Commands/Queries).
Docker containerization: Dockerfiles present for the deployable units (Fitnet/Src/Dockerfile, Fitnet.Contracts/Src/Dockerfile).
Visibility convention: classes are 'internal sealed' by default unless a named reason makes them public/open.
The solution contains both unit-test projects and integration-test projects (the combined testing strategy).
The Offers<->Passes integration coupling: Offers consumes the Passes integration event PassExpiredEvent over the message bus (asynchronous, not a synchronous in-process method call).
Project build config: static code analysis enabled (analyzers on, warnings-as-errors, code-style enforced in build) via Directory.Build.props.
The Contracts module's four Clean-Architecture layer projects (Api, Application, Infrastructure, Core)
The role of the Contracts.Core layer = domain model + essential business logic
The role of the Contracts.Application layer = use cases (command + handler pairs)
The role of the Contracts.Infrastructure layer = technical concerns (persistence, config, external integration, IO)
The role of the Contracts.Api layer = external communication (HTTP) / exposes the module's interface
The Clean-Architecture dependency rule for Contracts: Core is innermost and depends on nothing internal; dependencies point inward toward Core
The Passes module's two-project layered split: a Fitnet.Passes.Api project and a Fitnet.Passes.DataAccess project both exist.

The role split within the Passes module: the Api layer handles external communication (HTTP endpoints + event consumers) while the DataAccess layer holds persistence (PassesPersistence DbContext) plus the simple business logic (the Pass entity behavior).
The Reports module's architectural style: a simple read-model / transaction-script using raw SQL via Dapper over a single project, with no rich domain model.

A dedicated *.IntegrationEvents project per event-sending module, holding that module's integration-event definitions (Passes.IntegrationEvents holds PassExpiredEvent; Contracts.IntegrationEvents holds ContractSignedEvent).
The receiver -> sender's *.IntegrationEvents edge: Offers.Api references Passes.IntegrationEvents; Passes.Api references Contracts.IntegrationEvents (to reuse the event definitions).
Contracts implements Command-Query Separation using MediatR (ICommand : IRequest, IRequestHandler)
Config-driven module enable/disable: a 'Modules:<Name>:Enabled' flag checked at module registration (ModuleAvailabilityChecker.IsModuleEnabled).
Contracts extracted into a separate, independently-deployable microservice (own solution, own AppHost + Dockerfile + DB), no longer one of the monolith's in-process modules.
The monolith -> Contracts coupling: no project reference; the only coupling is Passes.Api consuming Contracts' integration events via the Contracts.IntegrationEvents PACKAGE (not a project).
The integration/cross-boundary transport mechanism: an out-of-process message queue (MassTransit over a broker), not an in-memory bus.
The message broker identity: RabbitMQ (MassTransit UsingRabbitMq + Aspire RabbitMQ hosting).
The event-bus mechanism: the out-of-process MassTransit/RabbitMQ bus is the only event bus (the in-memory bus discontinued).
The Inbox-Outbox reliable-messaging pattern with inbox/outbox state persisted in the database (Passes schema).
The Inbox-Outbox is implemented via MassTransit's built-in EntityFrameworkCore mechanism (AddEntityFrameworkOutbox), not hand-rolled.
Contracts uses the ErrorOr Result type for business-flow error handling instead of throwing exceptions for negative business scenarios
BindingContract is the Aggregate Root of the Contracts domain (Entity-derived, factory, owns the AttachedAnnexes child collection, records events)
Contracts' tactical-DDD building blocks: a shared Entity base class (with domain events) plus value objects / strongly-typed IDs (BindingContractId, ContractId, AnnexId)
Tests use Shouldly as the assertion library (the project migrated to Shouldly).

beyond_doc.note: BEYOND-DOC: no RC asserts Common is published/versioned as NuGet packages (RC-033 covers only the Entity base + typed IDs as DDD building blocks). scoring_provenance.method: isolated-agent scoring (separation of duties): 1 EC-extractor + 5 per-area matchers + 1 beyond-doc finder + per-overlap adversarial refuters (vs CODE), all blind to the curator's reasoning. Workflow  (34 agents).
scoring_provenance.orchestrator_caveat: The orchestrating session retained curator context (the reload did not start a fresh conversation), but every match/bucket/refutation JUDGMENT was made by an isolated agent blind to the curator's reasoning; the orchestrator only aggregated the machinery. A fully fresh scorer session is the cleaner ideal (recorded honestly).
scoring_provenance.rc008_reconciliation: RC-008 (Offers<->Passes async messaging) was matcher-MATCH (EC-022 correctly states the async chain) but the refuter flagged DeepInit's AGENTS.md architecture line ('talking over an in-process + RabbitMQ bus') as MEDIUM-imprecise for inter-module comms (they are RabbitMQ, not in-process). Conservatively DEMOTED MATCH->MISMATCH/deepinit_wrong/MEDIUM (so coverage is the conservative 26/33, not 27/33). MEDIUM -> does NOT trip the hard gate.

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness
